Title: v0.2.3 — Windows installer, welcome flow, cross-platform paths
Summary: Windows NSIS installer cross-built from Linux via a dedicated 64-bit Wine prefix. First-run welcome screen for packaged builds with no Godot project root yet — native folder picker via IPC instead of a broken-looking shell. Comprehensive Windows backslash path audit fixing `res://` construction, conversion, and client-side basename split. GitHub Pages landing page that auto-pulls the latest release from the GitHub API. Watcher-reimport bug fix discovered during the first Windows tester install.
Body:
## Headlines

### Windows NSIS installer

Second packaging target alongside the Linux AppImage. `pnpm dist:win` produces `electron/release/Bleepforge-Setup-<version>-x64.exe` (~86 MB) via electron-builder's NSIS target:

- **Full wizard** — welcome → install location → install (`nsis.oneClick: false`).
- **Per-user install** — no admin prompt needed (`nsis.perMachine: false`). App files land at `%LOCALAPPDATA%\Programs\Bleepforge\`; user state at `%APPDATA%\Bleepforge\data\` (mirrors the Linux `~/.config/Bleepforge/` convention via `app.setName("Bleepforge")`).
- **Desktop + start menu shortcuts** plus a working uninstaller registered in Add/Remove Programs.
- **Unsigned** — signing requires a paid certificate + EV-signing dance that isn't worth it for early testers. First install shows one SmartScreen warning; click *More info → Run anyway*.

A `.blockmap` ships alongside the installer for future auto-updater diffs (auto-update itself still deferred).

### Cross-build from Linux via 64-bit Wine

Windows builds can run on Linux hosts. electron-builder's `rcedit` step (sets `.exe` metadata + embeds the icon) shells out to `wine rcedit-x64.exe`, which requires a **64-bit Wine prefix** — a 32-bit prefix (the default `~/.wine`) fails with `wine: '/home/user/.wine' is a 32-bit installation, it cannot support 64-bit applications` and the build aborts after producing `win-unpacked/` but before the NSIS assembly.

`pnpm dist:win` defaults `WINEPREFIX` to `~/.wine64-bleepforge`. First-time setup is one command:

```bash
WINEPREFIX=~/.wine64-bleepforge WINEARCH=win64 \
  WINEDLLOVERRIDES='mscoree=;mshtml=' wineboot --init
```

`WINEDLLOVERRIDES` disables the Mono / Gecko install prompts that would otherwise hang a headless build — rcedit needs neither (it's a native PE editor). Keep the default `~/.wine` prefix untouched for other Wine apps.

### First-run welcome screen

Fresh installs (no `preferences.json` yet) previously rendered a broken-looking sidebar with empty pages — every fresh AppImage / Windows installer install (most acutely the Maor / Windows tester scenario) hit this. The welcome flow replaces that with a proper onboarding screen.

When `useGodotProjectRoot().saved === ""`, [App.tsx](/help/under-the-hood/app-shell) renders a centered `<WelcomeScreen>` instead of the routes shell. Single CTA:

- **Electron**: fires a new `dialog:pick-godot-folder` IPC that opens the OS-native folder picker.
- **Browser dev mode**: falls back to a text input for absolute paths.

The picked path goes through the existing `/api/godot-project/validate` endpoint for inline ✓/✗ feedback (does `project.godot` exist there?), then on Apply the prefs PUT lands with `keepalive: true` and the existing restart IPC fires so the server captures the new root at next boot.

The screen is intentionally **not a route** — it lives outside the React Router tree so deep-linked URLs like `/quests` still resolve correctly after the project root is set + the app restarts. Popouts skip the gate (they can't be opened from a non-functional main anyway).

### Cross-platform path handling — Windows backslash audit

Windows uses `\` separators; Linux / macOS use `/`. Node's `path` module produces native separators on each platform, so server-internal `path.join` / `path.resolve` survives both. Two patterns silently broke on Windows before this audit:

**`res://` path construction.** Three duplicated `absToResPath` helpers (in `textureRef.ts`, `domains/dialog.ts`, and the correct impl in `projectIndex/build.ts`) used `root.replace + substring + concat`. On Windows that produced `res://foo\bar.png` — invalid Godot path — AND failed the `startsWith(root + "/")` check entirely because Windows roots have `\` while the code appended `/`. Fixed by switching the two broken impls to `path.relative + replaceAll(path.sep, "/")`, matching the projectIndex impl. Godot's `res://` URI scheme is always forward-slashed regardless of host OS.

**`res://` → absolute conversion.** `resPathToAbs` used `root.replace + "/" + substring` which on Windows produced mixed-separator paths like `C:\root/foo/bar.png`. Fixed via `path.join` which normalizes.

**Client-side basename splits.** Server-produced paths come with backslashes on Windows; the browser has no `path` module. New helpers at [clientPath.ts](/help/under-the-hood/client-src-structure) — `lastPathSegment` + `dirOf` — split on either separator. Used by `AssetThumb` (context-menu label), `ImageEditor` (the Duplicate workflow was broken on Windows — `lastIndexOf("/")` returned -1 on backslash paths so `dir = ""` and the target-folder field rendered empty), and `WatcherTab` shorten-display.

Bleepforge-internal `<folder>/<basename>` id strings — NPC `CasualRemarks`, dialog folder keys, sync event keys — are always forward-slashed by convention and use plain `.split("/")` everywhere; only filesystem paths need the dual-separator helpers.

### GitHub Pages landing page

Bleepforge now has a website at https://ymoraviadev-droid.github.io/bleepforge/ via a new `docs/` folder published as the GitHub Pages source. Single-page pixel-themed landing with:

- Hero block (BLEEPFORGE wordmark + tagline + version chip).
- Two download buttons (Windows installer / Linux AppImage) that auto-populate from the most recent GitHub release via the `/releases?per_page=1` API endpoint (uses `per_page=1` instead of `/releases/latest` so pre-releases count too).
- Auto-rendered release notes from the latest tag's body, rendered via the `marked` library loaded from a CDN. Falls back to escaped plain text in a `<pre>` if the CDN failed to load.
- Static feature list + context section + footer with `mailto:` author credit.

Fallback `href`s in the HTML stay current even when the GitHub API call fails (rate limit, network) — so the page is always usable.

## Bug fixes

### Reimport `res://` post-pass — broken portraits caught on Maor's first Windows test

The boot reconcile orchestrator post-processes every texture field after the mapper runs — `item.Icon`, `faction.Icon` + `Banner`, `dialog.line.Portrait`, `npc.Portrait` — converting any remaining `res://...` strings to absolute paths via `resPathToAbs(root)`. The mappers themselves call `resPathToAbs(ext.path)` *without* a root, which falls back to `process.env.GODOT_PROJECT_ROOT` — unset whenever the actual root came from `preferences.json` (every welcome-flow install).

`reimportOne.ts` (the watcher's per-file path) was missing that post-pass entirely. Result: a Godot-side dialog edit → watcher reimport → JSON portraits became raw `res://characters/art/eddie.png` → asset router rejected them as "path outside allowed roots" → broken thumbnails until a Bleepforge restart re-ran the boot reconcile and rewrote the JSON correctly.

Same bug applied to item icons, faction icons + banners, NPC portraits — only dialog portraits surfaced because that's what Maor edited first. Fix: mirror the orchestrator's post-pass for every domain in the single-file reimport path.

> note: future cleanup will collapse the env-var fallback in `resPathToAbs` and force callers to pass `root` explicitly so the bug class can't recur.

## Known issues / deferred

- **macOS packaging** still not configured. `.dmg` is a config-only follow-up to the existing electron-builder pipeline.
- **Auto-update + code signing** still deferred until distribution is something other than "the user runs the installer from disk."
- **Genericize for any Godot project** — long-term anchor (v0.2.5+). The seven game-domain schemas + per-domain edit forms + per-domain `.tres` mappers are still hardcoded to Flock of Bleeps.

> tip: see [v0.2.2](/help/release-notes/v0-2-2) for the shader tabs + dirty guard + outgoing-toast work this release builds on.
